title: tetraneuris linearifolia var. arenicola
intro: Tetraneuris linearifolia var. arenicola, also known as sand marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the dry regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of sand marigold include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and preventing the common cold, as well as having antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile ingredient in traditional herbal medicine.
